courses.title: Tajweed & Deeniyat (2-Year)
courses.description: A foundational two-year program that builds fluent Quran recitation, essential Islamic knowledge, and daily worship practice for children and new learners.
courses.introduction: The Holy Qur'an is the final sacred message from Allah to humanity, revealed as a mercy, guidance, and a light for all mankind. The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best among you are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it.' This program is designed to help learners establish a strong foundation in proper Quranic recitation with tajweed, along with essential Islamic knowledge that guides daily worship and character development. Through structured lessons, daily practice, and caring mentorship, students will develop the skills and habits necessary to connect deeply with the Book of Allah.
targetAudience: Children aged 6-12 who want to begin their Quranic journey
New learners seeking a strong foundation in tajweed and Islamic knowledge
Anyone who wishes to enhance recitation accuracy and understanding
Parents looking for structured Islamic education for their children
Students who want to establish daily worship habits
curriculum: Noorani Qaida mastery
Makharij & Sifaat of letters
Core Tajweed rules (Ikhfa, Idgham, Qalqalah)
Surah memorization (Juz Amma)
Fiqh of Taharah and Salah
Essential duas and adhkar
Islamic manners and etiquette
rules: Minimum 85% attendance each term
Daily home revision log signed by guardians
Monthly oral assessment is mandatory
Respectful dress code and punctuality
Parents attend quarterly progress meetings

courses.title: Quran Memorization (3-Year)
courses.description: An intensive Hifz track focused on memorizing the entire Quran with strong tajweed, revision cycles, and personal mentorship.
courses.introduction: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The one who is proficient in the Qur'an is associated with the noble, scribes (angels); and he who recites the Qur'an and finds it difficult to recite, doing his best to recite it in the best way possible, will have a double reward.' (Bukhari & Muslim). In another hadith, the Prophet (ﷺ) stated: 'The Qur'an will meet its companion on the Day of Resurrection when his grave is opened for him, in the form of a pale man. It will ask him, 'Do you know who I am?' He will say, 'I do not know you.' It will say, 'I am your companion the Qur'an, who made you thirsty during the day and kept you awake at night. Every merchant will benefit from his trade, and today you will be enriched.' This intensive Hifz program is designed to guide dedicated students through the sacred journey of memorizing Allah's Book with proper tajweed, consistent revision, and spiritual connection.
targetAudience: Students with basic tajweed knowledge ready to commit to Hifz
Anyone who wishes to preserve the Qur'an in their heart
Dedicated learners who can commit to daily memorization routines
Students aged 10 and above with strong reading ability
Individuals seeking Allah's pleasure through Hifz
courses.schedule: Weekdays: 6:00 AM - 8:30 AM or 5:00 PM - 7:30 PM
features: Daily sabaq, sabqi, and manzil routines
curriculum: Revision of Juz Amma and Tabarak
Hifz targets for each quarter
Advanced Tajweed application
Weekly revision circles
Monthly comprehensive tasmi
Final Quran revision and consolidation
rules: Daily attendance is compulsory for Hifz success
Students must submit daily sabaq and sabqi
Parent/guardian receives weekly progress updates
Maintaining tajweed accuracy is required
Completion includes full Quran revision

courses.title: Ibadur Rahman (2-Year)
courses.description: A two-year boys' scholarship track focused on Arabic, Quran translation, and Islamic leadership skills for young learners.
courses.introduction: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A man is the guardian of his family and is responsible for them. A woman is the guardian of her husband's house and is responsible for it.' (Bukhari & Muslim). This hadith highlights the sacred responsibility men hold in leading and protecting their families. The Ibadur Rahman program is designed to shape young men into knowledgeable, God-conscious leaders who understand their roles in family, community, and society. Through comprehensive study of Arabic, Quranic sciences, Fiqh, Hadith, and practical leadership training, students will develop the intellectual foundation and moral character necessary to serve as true 'servants of the Most Merciful' - individuals who embody Islamic values in their personal lives and become positive influences in their communities.
targetAudience: Boys aged 13-18 seeking comprehensive Islamic education
Men who can dedicate two hours a day to learning and revision
Students aspiring to become community leaders and scholars
Young men wanting to understand Arabic and Quranic sciences
Individuals preparing for future roles in family and community service
curriculum: SEMESTER 1: Noorani Qaida (Tajweed basics - letter recognition and sounds), Urdu Reading & Writing (basic to intermediate), Fiqh Bahishti Zewar (Taharat - purification, wudu, ghusl, najasa), Aqeedah Imaniyat (Introduction to Islamic Beliefs - Tawhid, qualities of believers), Seerat (Introduction to Prophet's Life - Birth to Prophet hood)

SEMESTER 2: Tajweed (Makharij al-Huruf - articulation points & Sifaat al-Huruf - qualities of letters), Fiqh (Salah - conditions, pillars, wajib, sunnan), Quran Tafsir (Surah Yaseen - Part 1 - verses 1-60), Hadith Ma'ariful Hadith (Knowledge - Importance of seeking knowledge,Etiquettes of learning), Social Ethics (Islamic morals, respect for elders, kindness to peers)

SEMESTER 3: Tajweed (Idgham - merging, Ikhfa - hiding, Qalqalah - echoing), Fiqh (Fasting - Ramadan rules, sawm types, fidya,kaffarah), Quran Tafsir (Surah Yaseen complete, Surah Rahman - Mercy of Allah), Hadith (Trials - Faith and testing, perseverance in difficulties), Seerat (Prophet's Life - Migration to Madinah, building the Muslim community)

SEMESTER 4: Tajweed (Advanced rules - Madd, Hamzatul Wasl, Laam Ta'reef), Fiqh (Zakat - rules and calculation, Hajj - ihram, tawaf, sa'i, rites), Quran Tafsir (Surah Waqiah - The Event, Surah Mulk - Sovereignty), Hadith (Qiyamah - Signs of Hour, Day of Judgment, World - Parables and warnings), Marriage & Family Life (Rights of spouse, parenting, family harmony), Leadership Skills (Public speaking, organizing, community responsibility)
rules: Minimum 85% attendance each semester is mandatory
Daily revision of at least 30 minutes required for Quran and subjects
Weekly homework submission for Arabic and Urdu
Monthly written examination covering all subjects
Termly oral assessment for Quran recitation and Hadith memorization
Participation in weekly khidmah (community service) is required
Notebook and homework checks every fortnight
Termly oral and written examinations
Punctual attendance and adab (etiquette) expected at all times
Parents attend bi-annual student reviews and progress meetings
Modest dress code adhering to Islamic guidelines
Respectful behavior towards teachers and peers required

courses.title: Momina Program (2-Year)
courses.description: A holistic two-year program for women covering Quran recitation, essential fiqh, family life, and spiritual development.
courses.introduction: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Seeking knowledge is obligatory upon every Muslim.' (Ibn Majah) and 'Whoever follows a path in pursuit of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise.' (Muslim). The wisdom of Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) exemplifies the heights of scholarship women can achieve. The Momina Program is dedicated to empowering women with authentic Islamic knowledge, practical guidance for family life, and spiritual development. Our female-led program creates a supportive sisterhood environment where women can strengthen their connection with the Qur'an, understand their rights and responsibilities in Islam, and develop the skills to nurture God-conscious families and communities.
targetAudience: Women seeking comprehensive Islamic education from female scholars
Women who can dedicate two hours a day to learning and revision
Mothers wanting to raise children with Islamic values
Young women preparing for marriage and family life
Professionals and homemakers seeking spiritual growth and knowledge
curriculum: SEMESTER 1: Noorani Qaida (Tajweed basics - letter recognition, basic harakat), Urdu Reading & Writing (Foundations), Fiqh (Taharat - wudu, ghusl, taharah, menstrual hygiene), Aqeedah (Fundamentals of Iman - Tawhid, Belief in Allah and His Angels), Seerat (Prophet's Life - Selected events, Female companions)

SEMESTER 2: Tajweed (Makharij & Sifaat - proper articulation), Fiqh (Salah - women's obligations, congregation, prayer at home), Tafsir (Surah Yaseen - Introduction and selected verses), Hadith (Ma'ariful Hadith selections on worship and character), Islamic Etiquette (Adab towards parents, neighbors, and society)

SEMESTER 3: Tajweed (Idgham, Ikhfa, Qalqalah rules), Fiqh (Fasting - Ramadan, types of fasts, exemptions for women), Tafsir (Surah Rahman - Divine Mercy, understanding Allah's blessings), Hadith ( selections on family rights, treating relatives), Family Rights (Rights of wife, husband, children, extended family)
Women in Islam (Women's rights, roles, and responsibilities, historical examples)

SEMESTER 4: Tajweed (Advanced application - Madd, stopping rules), Fiqh (Zakat - women's obligations, Hajj - women's rules and conditions, Marriage - nikah, mahr, rights of spouses), Tafsir (Surah Al-Kahf - lessons for women), Hadith ( selections on parenting, child rearing), Child Rearing (Islamic guidelines for raising children, teaching duas and salah), Home Management (Organizing household, time management for students and mothers)
rules: Minimum 80% attendance per semester is mandatory
Daily revision of at least 30 minutes required for Quran and course materials
Weekly reflection journal submission
Monthly written examination covering all subjects
Semester assessments including both written and oral exams
Group projects on family life themes (twice per semester)
Weekly revision assignments to be submitted on time
Classroom etiquette and modest dress code must be upheld at all times
Participation in sisterhood circle discussions
Parents or guardians attend semester progress meetings
Conduct must reflect Islamic values and adab
Attendance at special seminars and workshops conducted during the program

courses.title: Bachelor of Alimiyat (5-Year)
courses.description: A comprehensive five-year scholarship program in classical Islamic sciences, designed to train future scholars and teachers.
courses.introduction: Every era requires scholars who understand the time they live in, are deeply rooted in Islamic tradition, and can address contemporary challenges with wisdom and knowledge. The Prophet (ﷺ) emphasized the importance of scholars: 'The scholars are the inheritors of the Prophets.' (Tirmidhi) and 'Verily, Allah does not take away knowledge by snatching it from the people, but He takes it away by taking away the scholars.' (Bukhari & Muslim). This rigorous five-year Alimiyat program follows the classical Dars-e-Nizami curriculum, training students in Quranic exegesis, Hadith, Fiqh, Arabic sciences, and Islamic theology. Our graduates emerge as qualified scholars, teachers, and community leaders equipped to serve the Ummah with authentic knowledge, sound understanding, and practical wisdom.
targetAudience: Students aspiring to become Islamic scholars and teachers
Professionals seeking advanced Islamic education alongside careers
Homemakers wanting comprehensive knowledge for family and community service
Individuals preparing for roles in Islamic institutions and academia
Anyone committed to deep study of Islamic sciences
curriculum: YEAR 1 (Semesters 1-2): Arabic Grammar (Asan Nahw - basic sentence structure), Arabic Morphology (Sharh Mi'at Aamil - 100 particles), Fiqh (Bahishti Zewar complete - all aspects of worship and daily life), Seerah (Qisas Al-Nabiyyin - Stories of the Prophets), Tajweed (Rules application and Quran recitation), Hifz (Juz Amma memorization with tajweed), Islamic Ethics (Akhlaq and adab)

YEAR 2 (Semesters 3-4): Sarf (Advanced Morphology - derived forms, Patterns), Qirat (Quran Recitation variants - different qira'at basics), Fiqh (Hidayah - Taharat and Salah in detail), Tafsir (Surah Al-Baqarah selected verses - tafsir al-mizan selections), Hadith (Sahih al-Bukhari selections - approximately 100 hadith), Usool al-Tafsir (Introduction to Quranic exegesis methods)

YEAR 3 (Semesters 5-6): Nahw (Advanced Grammar - kana and sisters, inna and sisters), Fiqh (Fasting, Zakat, Hajj - detailed rulings), Tafsir (Surah Al-Baqarah continuation, Surah Ale Imran - detailed tafsir), Hadith (Mishkat al-Masabih - selections on faith and worship), Usool al-Tafsir (Advanced tafsir methodologies, asbab al-nuzul), Qira'at (Practical recitation of different qira'at)

YEAR 4 (Semesters 7-8): Fiqh (Advanced - Mu'amalat (transactions), Nikah (marriage), Judicial rulings), Usool al-Fiqh (Principles of Islamic jurisprudence - adillah, qiyas, ijma', etc.), Arabic Literature (Selected prose and poetry from classical sources), Islamic History (Caliphs and major events), Comparative Religions (Islam vs other faiths - theological discussions), Qira'at (Advanced practice and theory)

YEAR 5 (Semesters 9-10): Fiqh (Contemporary Issues - modern banking, bioethics, social issues), Khulafa al-Rashidin History (Detailed study of the Rightly Guided Caliphs), Teaching Methodology (Dars preparation, classroom management, student assessment), Research Methods (Academic writing, thesis preparation, citing sources), Ijazah Preparation (Seeking authorization to teach, chain of transmission), Islamic Finance (Basic principles of Islamic banking and economics)
rules: Minimum 90% attendance required for all lectures and sessions
Daily mutala'ah (study) of at least 2 hours beyond class time mandatory
Weekly written assignments for each major subject
Monthly examination covering subjects studied in that month
Annual comprehensive examinations at end of each academic year
Mid-year and end-of-year oral examinations ( viva voce)
Participation in teaching practicum from Year 3 onwards (minimum 50 hours)
Adherence to institute code of conduct at all times on campus
Maintain respectful dress code appropriate for Islamic scholars
Attend weekly jum'ah prayer and religious gatherings
Complete research project/thesis in final year under faculty supervision
No more than 2 unexcused absences per month allowed
All students must serve as teaching assistants for at least one semester
Participation in community outreach and dawah activities required
Regular attendance at weekly halaqas and circles of knowledge
